sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.6 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Is it true that Golod$p$-groups are non-amenable? These infinite finitely generated torsion groups are defined in (E.\,S.\,Golod, Amer. Math. Soc. Transl. (2), 48 (1965), 103--106). { P.~de~la~Harpe} Yes, moreover, such groups have infinite quotients with Kazhdan's property~(T) and are uniformly non-amenable (M.\,Ershov, A.\,Jaikin-Zapirain, , { 102}, no.\,4 (2011), 599--636)...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
